metadata.note: Output used as-is without manual revision or selection.

causal_texts: The glass slipped from her hand. It fell to the floor. It broke into many pieces. She swept them up carefully.
Daniel forgot to set his alarm before sleeping. He woke after the morning bus had departed. He hurried outside and called a nearby taxi. The driver delivered him to work just before noon.

Maya planted tomato seeds in a sunny garden bed. She watered the soil every evening after dinner. Small green shoots appeared during the following week. By summer, she harvested several baskets of ripe tomatoes.

A storm knocked a branch onto the power line. The neighborhood suddenly lost electricity during the evening. Residents lit candles and unplugged sensitive electronic devices. Repair crews restored power before the next morning.

Leo spilled coffee across his printed presentation notes. The ink blurred and several pages became unreadable. He reopened the document and printed another copy. He reached the meeting with clean notes in hand.
Nina heard scratching sounds behind the kitchen wall. She placed a harmless trap near the cupboard. A small mouse entered the trap during the night. Nina released the mouse in a distant field.
The bicycle tire went flat during Omar's commute. He walked the bicycle to a nearby repair shop. A mechanic replaced the punctured inner tube. Omar continued riding and arrived home safely.
Priya mixed flour, yeast, salt, and warm water. She kneaded the dough until it became smooth. The dough rose beside the warm kitchen window. She baked a fragrant loaf for the family dinner.
A child left the freezer door slightly open. Warm air entered and melted several ice cubes. Water dripped onto the kitchen floor overnight. His father mopped the puddle before breakfast.
Elena noticed dark clouds gathering above the park. She packed the picnic food into a basket. Rain began as everyone reached the parking lot. The family drove home and ate indoors.
Marcus dropped his keys through a narrow sidewalk grate. He borrowed a magnet and some strong string. The magnet caught the metal key ring below. Marcus pulled the keys back through the grate.

The classroom projector stopped working before the lecture. Professor Lee checked the cables and found one loose. She pushed the connector firmly into the port. The presentation appeared clearly on the screen.
A puppy chewed through the garden hose. Water sprayed across the patio and flowerpots. Carla closed the outdoor valve near the wall. She replaced the damaged hose the next day.
Jonah left wet clothes inside the washing machine. A sour smell developed during the warm afternoon. He washed the clothes again with detergent. Then he hung everything outside in fresh air.
The hikers followed a trail marked with blue signs. One sign had fallen behind a dense bush. They took the wrong path and reached a stream. A ranger guided them back to the main trail.
Grace found an old camera in her attic. She cleaned the lens and inserted fresh batteries. The camera powered on after several quiet clicks. Grace photographed the sunset from her balcony.

A restaurant received more guests than expected that evening. The kitchen quickly ran low on fresh bread. A cook prepared extra dough between other orders. Warm rolls reached the tables before the main courses.
Henry heard the smoke alarm chirping every minute. He brought a ladder from the garage. He replaced the weak battery in the alarm. The annoying chirp stopped immediately afterward.

Sofia opened a package containing a cracked ceramic bowl. She photographed the damage and contacted the seller. The seller approved a replacement without additional charge. A new bowl arrived safely three days later.
A soccer ball rolled into a muddy drainage ditch. Amir found a long stick beside the field. He pushed the ball toward the shallow bank. His teammate grabbed it and cleaned the surface.

Clara left her umbrella on the crowded train. She noticed the mistake after reaching the station exit. She reported the missing umbrella to station staff. An employee returned it from the lost property office.

The printer ran out of paper during a busy afternoon. Evan opened the storage cabinet beside the desk. He loaded a fresh stack into the tray. The waiting documents printed without further interruption.
A candle tipped over beside a stack of newspapers. Rosa quickly smothered the small flame with a blanket. She carried the scorched papers outside afterward. The room suffered no serious damage.

Ben noticed his phone battery dropping unusually fast. He closed several applications running in the background. The battery drain slowed during the next hour. Ben reached home before the phone turned off.
A gardener found aphids covering several rose stems. She sprayed the stems with mild soapy water. The aphid population decreased over the following days. New rose buds opened without visible damage.
Liam missed a step while carrying a box upstairs. The box slipped and landed on the hallway rug. Several books scattered across the floor. Liam gathered them and finished the move carefully.
A cat knocked a plant from the windowsill. Soil spread across the carpet below the window. Keiko moved the cat into another room. She vacuumed the dirt and repotted the plant.
Sam entered the wrong password several times. The website temporarily locked his account. He followed the emailed recovery instructions. A new password restored his access within minutes.

A delivery truck blocked the narrow street outside school. Traffic began backing up toward the intersection. A police officer directed the driver into a loading area. Cars started moving normally again.
Mei placed a metal spoon inside the microwave. Bright sparks flashed as the machine started. She stopped the microwave immediately and removed the spoon. The food reheated safely in a ceramic bowl.

Cold weather froze the water inside an exposed pipe. The expanding ice cracked the pipe near a joint. Water leaked when temperatures rose the next morning. A plumber replaced the damaged section that afternoon.

Alex found a wallet beneath a library chair. He checked the identification card for the owner's name. A librarian announced the name over the speaker. The relieved owner collected the wallet at the desk.

The band arrived early for the outdoor concert. Strong wind began shaking the stage decorations. Crew members secured the decorations with extra ropes. The concert started safely at the scheduled time.
Tara forgot a cake inside the hot oven. A burnt smell soon filled the apartment. She turned off the oven and opened windows. Fresh air cleared the smoke before guests arrived.
A loose screw made the chair wobble badly. Miguel found a screwdriver in the kitchen drawer. He tightened each screw beneath the seat. The chair stood firmly on the floor afterward.
Jasmine saw frost covering her car windshield. She started the heater and scraped the glass. The frost gradually disappeared from every window. She drove away with a clear view.

A student accidentally deleted an important essay file. He checked the computer's recycle folder immediately. The missing file remained there with its original name. He restored it and saved an extra backup.
Wind carried several papers from Ava's outdoor table. She chased them across the empty courtyard. A fence stopped the last page near the gate. Ava collected every sheet and used a paperweight.
The family dog escaped through an open backyard gate. Neighbors spotted the dog near a grocery store. They called the phone number printed on its collar. The family retrieved the dog before sunset.
A sudden sneeze made Victor drop his paintbrush. Blue paint splashed across the white floorboards. He wiped the paint before it dried. The floor remained clean without any visible stain.
Rina noticed her soup tasted far too salty. She added water and several chopped potatoes. The potatoes absorbed some salt while cooking. The finished soup tasted balanced enough to serve.

The office air conditioner failed during a heat wave. Employees opened windows and moved portable fans inside. A technician replaced the broken motor that afternoon. The office cooled down before the evening shift.

George left his bicycle unlocked outside the bakery. He remembered after ordering bread at the counter. He rushed outside and found the bicycle untouched. George locked it securely before returning inside.

A child pressed every button on the television remote. The screen switched to an unfamiliar input source. Her sister selected the correct input from the menu. The usual program appeared on the screen again.
Yuki heard water running inside the bathroom wall. He closed the apartment's main water valve. A plumber opened the wall and found a leak. The repaired pipe stopped wasting water.

The baker measured too little sugar for the cookie dough. She tasted a small baked test cookie. The cookie tasted bland compared with her recipe. She added more sugar before baking the remaining batch.
Noah stepped into a deep puddle on his way home. Water soaked through his shoes and socks. He changed into dry clothes after arriving. Then he placed the wet shoes beside a heater.

A bookstore received a shipment with damaged cardboard boxes. Staff inspected every book before shelving them. They separated several copies with bent covers. The distributor later replaced all damaged copies.

The train stopped because debris covered part of the track. Workers removed the fallen branches and checked the rails. The driver received permission to continue. Passengers reached the next station after a short delay.

Isabel found her favorite necklace tangled in a drawer. She placed it flat under a bright lamp. Using two needles, she loosened the smallest knot. The necklace became wearable again without any broken links.
A chef noticed smoke rising from an empty pan. He removed the pan from the burner immediately. The kitchen staff opened doors for ventilation. The pan cooled without causing a fire.
A strong gust slammed the garden gate shut. The latch jammed and trapped Paul outside. He called his daughter through an open window. She released the latch from inside the yard.
Fatima forgot to bring lunch to the office. She checked a nearby cafe's menu online. She ordered a vegetable sandwich for pickup. The sandwich was ready during her lunch break.
A boy kicked a ball against the garage door. The ball struck a shelf holding paint cans. One can fell and opened on the concrete. His parents covered the spill with absorbent material.
The refrigerator made a loud rattling sound overnight. Ana moved it slightly away from the wall. A loose tray stopped touching the rear panel. The refrigerator became quiet for the rest of the night.
A traveler noticed her passport missing at the airport. She searched the security tray area with an officer. The passport lay beneath a folded jacket. She reached her gate before boarding closed.
A tree root lifted one corner of the garden path. Chen tripped but avoided serious injury. He marked the raised stone with bright tape. A worker leveled the path the following morning.
Olivia poured too much soap into the washing machine. Thick foam began leaking from the door. She stopped the cycle and removed some suds. A rinse cycle cleared the remaining soap.

The theater lights failed during the opening scene. Actors paused while technicians checked the electrical panel. A tripped breaker was reset backstage. The performance resumed after a brief announcement.
Ken left a window open before a dusty windstorm. Fine dust covered the desk and bookshelves. He closed the window and wore a mask. Then he wiped every surface with a damp cloth.
A child built a tall tower from wooden blocks. The table shook when someone closed the door. The tower collapsed into a noisy pile. The child started again on the floor.
The camping stove would not ignite in the morning. Lena checked the fuel canister and found it empty. She attached a full replacement canister. Hot water soon boiled for coffee.

A mechanic heard a clicking noise from the engine. He inspected the compartment and found a loose belt. He adjusted the tension and tested the vehicle. The clicking noise disappeared during the test drive.
Rachel overwatered a small cactus on her desk. The soil stayed wet and the roots softened. She removed the damaged roots and changed the soil. The cactus recovered near a sunny window.
A bird flew into the open garage. It circled above the parked car nervously. Martin opened both doors and turned off the lights. The bird followed daylight and flew outside.
A musician broke a guitar string during rehearsal. She opened her case and found a spare string. She installed and tuned the replacement carefully. Rehearsal continued with only a short pause.
A snowplow blocked Emma's driveway with a heavy ridge. She used a shovel to break the packed snow. Her neighbor helped move the largest chunks. Emma drove to work after clearing a narrow path.
The hotel guest heard loud music next door. He called the front desk and explained the problem. Staff contacted the neighboring room politely. The music became quiet within several minutes.
A child left crayons on a sunny car seat. The crayons softened and melted into colorful streaks. His mother cooled the wax with ice. She scraped it away without damaging the fabric.

Raj noticed a strange smell near the gas stove. He turned off every burner and opened windows. A technician found a loose connection in the supply line. The technician repaired it before the stove was used again.

A library book became wet inside Mia's backpack. Her water bottle had opened during the walk. She dried each page with absorbent paper. The librarian accepted the book after inspecting the minor damage.
A small boat drifted away from the dock. Its rope had slipped from a metal cleat. A fisherman reached the boat with a kayak. He secured it using a stronger knot.

A power surge restarted every computer in the office. Several unsaved documents disappeared from open screens. Employees recovered recent versions from automatic backups. The manager ordered surge protectors for every workstation.
A fox entered the chicken enclosure through a gap. The hens made loud noises before dawn. The farmer scared the fox away with a flashlight. He repaired the fence before nightfall.
Camila dropped an earring into the bathroom sink. She closed the drain before running any water. Her brother removed the curved pipe underneath. The missing earring appeared inside the pipe.
A child touched wet paint on the hallway wall. Red paint covered his fingers and shirt. His father washed his hands with soap. The stained shirt went directly into the laundry.
The apartment key snapped inside the front lock. Julia called a locksmith from the hallway. The locksmith removed the broken metal piece. A replacement key opened the door smoothly.
A strong breeze blew the tablecloth toward a candle. The cloth began to smoke near one corner. Malik pulled it away and extinguished the ember. He moved the candle to a safer shelf.
The grocery bag tore while Petra crossed the street. Oranges rolled toward the curb in several directions. Two pedestrians helped gather the fruit. Petra carried everything home in a spare bag.

A student left his calculator at home before an exam. He asked the teacher about borrowing one. The teacher provided a spare from the supply cabinet. He completed every calculation before time expired.
A leak formed under the kitchen sink. Water collected inside the wooden cabinet. Denise placed a bucket beneath the pipe. A plumber tightened the loose connection that afternoon.
A kite became tangled in a low tree branch. Children pulled the string but worsened the knot. An adult used a ladder to reach the branch. The kite came down with only a small tear.
Arun cooked rice without enough water. The bottom burned while the top remained hard. He discarded the damaged batch and cleaned the pot. A second batch cooked properly with measured water.
The museum visitor leaned too close to an alarm sensor. A warning tone sounded throughout the gallery. A guard asked everyone to step back. The alarm stopped after the sensor area cleared.
A car splashed muddy water onto Helen's coat. She reached the office with several brown stains. She treated the fabric with stain remover. The coat looked clean after an evening wash.
Thomas noticed ants crossing his kitchen counter. He sealed an open sugar container. Then he cleaned crumbs from every surface. The ant trail disappeared within two days.
A sudden gust blew the tent flap open. Rain entered and dampened the sleeping bags. Campers closed the flap and moved supplies inward. They dried the bags beside a small fire.
An ice cube blocked the narrow bottle opening. Warm water loosened the ice around the edge. The cube slipped into the bottle after a minute. Clara filled the bottle without further trouble.
A package arrived at the wrong apartment door. The resident recognized the neighbor's name on the label. She carried the box upstairs and knocked. The grateful neighbor accepted the unopened package.
Diego forgot sunscreen during a long beach afternoon. His shoulders became red and painful by evening. He applied cool gel after returning home. The discomfort eased while he rested indoors.
A loose shoelace caught under Maria's foot. She stumbled near the station entrance. A passerby helped her regain balance. Maria tied both laces before continuing.
The coffee machine displayed an error before opening time. Staff emptied the full grounds container. They cleaned the sensor and restarted the machine. Customers received coffee without a long delay.
A ball struck the greenhouse window during practice. A thin crack spread across the glass. The coach covered the area with protective tape. A glazier replaced the pane the next morning.
Haruto left a paper map on the car roof. The map blew away when he started driving. He stopped and walked back along the road. Haruto found it caught against a roadside bush.
A heavy book fell behind the bedroom radiator. Sophie could not reach it with her hand. She slid a ruler beneath the narrow gap. The ruler pushed the book within reach.
The freezer accumulated a thick layer of ice. Drawers became difficult to open fully. Luis unplugged the appliance and removed the food. Melted ice drained away before he restarted it.
A swimmer noticed his goggles filling with water. He returned to the pool edge and adjusted the strap. The seal fit tightly around both eyes. He resumed swimming with clear vision.
A mailbox key disappeared from Nora's key ring. She searched her coat pockets and desk drawers. The key was inside a grocery bag. Nora returned it to the ring immediately.
A cyclist heard thunder while riding through open countryside. She turned toward the nearest public shelter. Heavy rain began moments after she arrived. She waited indoors until the storm passed.

An old shelf sagged under several heavy dictionaries. One bracket began pulling away from the wall. Isaac removed the books and replaced the screws. The strengthened shelf held the books securely afterward.
noncausal_texts: A violin is played with a bow. A flute is played by blowing air. A drum is played by striking it. A harp is played by plucking strings.
Maple trees produce winged seeds called samaras. Pine trees carry seeds inside woody cones. Willow trees often grow near streams and wetlands. Birch trees usually have thin, papery bark.
Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun. Saturn has a prominent system of icy rings. Mars has two small natural satellites. Venus has a thick atmosphere rich in carbon dioxide.
Owls have forward-facing eyes adapted for depth perception. Ducks have webbed feet suited for swimming. Eagles use powerful talons to capture prey. Hummingbirds can hover while feeding from flowers.
A triangle has three straight sides. A circle contains no corners or straight edges. A rectangle has four right angles. A pentagon has five sides.
Carrots store nutrients in enlarged taproots. Peas grow inside elongated green pods. Lettuce forms clusters of edible leaves. Pumpkins develop from flowers on creeping vines.
A thermometer measures temperature. A barometer measures atmospheric pressure. A hygrometer measures humidity in the air. An anemometer measures wind speed.
Granite commonly contains quartz, feldspar, and mica. Marble forms when limestone undergoes heat and pressure. Basalt is a dark volcanic rock. Sandstone consists mainly of cemented sand grains.

Japanese uses several writing systems in ordinary text. Arabic is written from right to left. Spanish commonly marks questions with opening and closing symbols. Korean writing uses an alphabet called Hangul.
A cello is larger than a standard violin. A trumpet produces sound through vibrating lips. A clarinet usually uses a single reed. Timpani can be tuned to specific pitches.
Bees collect nectar and pollen from flowers. Ants live in organized colonies with specialized roles. Butterflies have scales covering their wings. Dragonflies possess two pairs of transparent wings.
A compass indicates directions using Earth's magnetic field. A ruler measures length along a straight edge. A protractor measures angles in degrees. A balance compares masses or measures weight.
Aluminum is lightweight and resistant to corrosion. Copper conducts electricity and heat efficiently. Iron can form rust in moist air. Gold is highly malleable and chemically unreactive.

The Pacific Ocean contains many volcanic island chains. The Arctic Ocean surrounds the North Pole. The Indian Ocean borders eastern Africa. The Atlantic Ocean separates the Americas from Europe and Africa.
Clouds consist of tiny water droplets or ice crystals. Fog forms near the ground and reduces visibility. Hail consists of layered balls of ice. Snowflakes develop as ice crystals inside cold clouds.

A novel presents an extended fictional narrative. A dictionary explains meanings and usages of words. An atlas contains maps of geographic regions. A cookbook provides instructions for preparing food.

Kidneys filter waste products from the blood. Lungs exchange oxygen and carbon dioxide. The liver performs many metabolic and detoxification functions. The heart pumps blood through the circulatory system.
A square has four equal sides. An ellipse has two focal points. A rhombus has four equal sides. A trapezoid has at least one pair of parallel sides.
Penguins have wings modified into swimming flippers. Ostriches are flightless birds with powerful legs. Parrots often have curved beaks and grasping feet. Swans have long necks and broad webbed feet.
Rice is a staple grain in many cuisines. Lentils are edible seeds from legume plants. Oats are commonly rolled or ground before eating. Corn kernels grow in rows on a cob.

A microscope magnifies objects too small for unaided vision. A telescope gathers light from distant objects. Binoculars provide magnified views through two optical tubes. A periscope uses mirrors or prisms to redirect sight.

Quartz crystals can produce voltage under mechanical stress. Diamond consists of carbon atoms in a rigid lattice. Graphite conducts electricity along its layered structure. Salt crystals commonly form cubic shapes.

A library organizes materials for reading and research. A museum preserves objects of cultural or scientific interest. A theater provides space for live performances. A laboratory supports controlled experiments and measurements.

Frogs absorb some water through their skin. Turtles protect their bodies with bony shells. Snakes smell partly by collecting particles with their tongues. Lizards typically have movable eyelids and external ear openings.
Blueberries grow on woody shrubs. Strawberries carry many small fruits on their outer surface. Bananas develop in clusters on large herbaceous plants. Grapes grow in bunches on woody vines.

A keyboard enters letters, numbers, and commands. A monitor displays visual information from a computer. A router directs data between computer networks. A printer creates physical copies of digital documents.

Earth rotates around an axis passing through both poles. The Moon reflects sunlight rather than producing visible light. Comets contain ice, dust, and rocky material. Asteroids are mostly rocky or metallic bodies orbiting the Sun.
Soap helps water remove oils from surfaces. Vinegar contains acetic acid dissolved in water. Baking soda is sodium bicarbonate. Bleach can disinfect many nonporous household surfaces.
A sonnet traditionally contains fourteen lines. A haiku commonly uses three short lines. An epic poem narrates heroic or legendary events. A limerick often follows a distinctive rhythmic pattern.

Salmon can migrate between fresh water and seawater. Sharks have skeletons made mainly of cartilage. Seahorse males carry developing young in a pouch. Tuna are streamlined fish adapted for sustained swimming.
A violin has four strings in standard tuning. A piano produces sound when hammers strike strings. A trombone changes pitch using a movable slide. A xylophone uses tuned bars arranged across a frame.
Glass is usually made primarily from silica. Paper commonly consists of compressed cellulose fibers. Rubber can stretch and recover its original shape. Concrete combines cement, aggregate, and water.

Red blood cells transport oxygen using hemoglobin. White blood cells participate in immune defenses. Platelets help form clots after blood vessel injury. Plasma carries dissolved substances through the bloodstream.
A cactus stores water in thickened tissues. Ferns reproduce using spores rather than seeds. Mosses lack true vascular tissue. Orchids produce flowers with highly specialized structures.
France uses the euro as its currency. Japan consists of thousands of islands. Brazil occupies a large part of South America. Egypt contains most of the lower Nile Valley.
A lever rotates around a fixed point called a fulcrum. A pulley guides a rope around a grooved wheel. A wedge separates material using inclined surfaces. A screw converts rotation into linear force.
Robins often forage for insects on lawns. Woodpeckers use stiff tail feathers for support. Pelicans catch fish with expandable throat pouches. Falcons have pointed wings suited for fast flight.

Milk contains water, proteins, fats, and dissolved sugars. Cheese forms through controlled coagulation of milk proteins. Yogurt is produced by bacterial fermentation of milk. Butter contains concentrated milk fat.
A noun can name a person, place, object, or idea. A verb commonly expresses an action or state. An adjective modifies a noun or pronoun. An adverb often modifies a verb, adjective, or adverb.

Volcanoes release molten rock, gases, and ash. Earthquakes produce seismic waves through sudden crustal movement. Glaciers slowly flow under their own weight. Rivers transport sediment toward lakes or oceans.
A hammer drives nails or delivers focused impacts. Pliers grip, bend, or cut small objects. A saw cuts material with a toothed edge. A wrench turns nuts, bolts, and fittings.

Coffee beans are seeds found inside coffee fruits. Tea leaves come from the plant Camellia sinensis. Cocoa powder comes from processed cacao seeds. Cinnamon comes from the inner bark of certain trees.

A prism separates white light into different wavelengths. A mirror reflects light from a polished surface. A lens bends light through refraction. A diffraction grating separates light using closely spaced grooves.
Whales breathe air through blowholes. Dolphins use echolocation to navigate and hunt. Seals have streamlined bodies and paddlelike limbs. Walruses possess long tusks and sensitive whiskers.
An equilateral triangle has three equal sides. A right triangle contains one ninety-degree angle. An isosceles triangle has at least two equal sides. A scalene triangle has three unequal sides.
Redwood trees can grow extremely tall. Baobab trees store water in thick trunks. Mangrove trees tolerate salty coastal environments. Olive trees can survive in dry Mediterranean climates.

A refrigerator slows food spoilage by maintaining low temperatures. An oven heats food inside an enclosed chamber. A blender uses rotating blades to mix ingredients. A toaster browns sliced bread using radiant heat.
DNA stores hereditary information in many organisms. RNA performs several roles in gene expression. Proteins consist of chains of amino acids. Lipids include fats, oils, and membrane components.

Venus rotates very slowly compared with Earth. Jupiter has a strong magnetic field. Neptune appears blue because of atmospheric absorption and scattering. Uranus rotates with an unusually tilted axis.
A violin bow commonly uses horsehair under tension. A guitar fret divides the neck into pitch positions. A piano keyboard includes black and white keys. A drumhead vibrates when struck.

Oak wood has visible growth rings and prominent grain. Bamboo is a grass with hollow segmented stems. Cork comes from the outer bark of cork oak trees. Plywood consists of thin wood layers bonded together.

A passport identifies a traveler across national borders. A ticket grants access to transportation or an event. A map represents locations and spatial relationships. A suitcase stores belongings during travel.

A glacier contains compacted ice formed from accumulated snow. A desert receives very little precipitation. A wetland has water-saturated soil for substantial periods. A savanna combines grasses with scattered trees.

Chess uses pieces with different movement rules. Checkers uses diagonal moves on a patterned board. Go uses black and white stones on grid intersections. Backgammon combines dice rolls with strategic piece movement.
A magnet has north and south magnetic poles. Electric current creates a magnetic field. Opposite electric charges attract each other. Identical electric charges repel each other.
Lemons contain citric acid and aromatic oils. Cucumbers have high water content. Eggplants belong to the nightshade family. Garlic grows as a bulb divided into cloves.

A violinist changes pitch by pressing strings against the fingerboard. A flutist changes notes using keys and breath control. A drummer controls tone through striking position and force. A harpist selects notes by plucking individual strings.

Coral reefs support diverse marine communities. Kelp forests grow in cool nutrient-rich coastal waters. Seagrass meadows provide habitat in shallow seas. Mangrove roots shelter young fish near tropical shorelines.

A compass rose displays cardinal and intermediate directions. Contour lines connect points of equal elevation. A map scale relates map distance to ground distance. A legend explains symbols used on a map.

Saturn is less dense than liquid water on average. Mars contains the large volcano Olympus Mons. Mercury experiences extreme temperature differences between day and night. Earth has abundant liquid water at its surface.
Silk fibers come from silkworm cocoons. Cotton fibers grow around seeds inside plant bolls. Wool fibers come from animal fleece. Linen fibers come from flax plant stems.
An atom contains a nucleus surrounded by electrons. A proton carries positive electric charge. A neutron has no net electric charge. An electron carries negative electric charge.
Crows can solve some multi-step problems. Pigeons navigate using several environmental cues. Sparrows commonly build nests near human structures. Herons hunt aquatic prey in shallow water.

Mountains influence rainfall by forcing air upward. Valleys form low areas between higher landforms. Plateaus have elevated surfaces with relatively flat terrain. Canyons have steep sides carved by erosion.
A bicycle uses pedals to turn a chain-driven wheel. A sailboat moves by capturing wind with sails. A train travels along fixed rails. A helicopter generates lift with rotating blades.

Potatoes are underground storage stems called tubers. Onions are layered bulbs with a strong aroma. Asparagus produces edible young shoots. Broccoli consists mainly of immature flower clusters and stems.

Sound waves require a material medium in ordinary conditions. Light can travel through empty space. Water waves transfer energy across a fluid surface. Seismic waves travel through Earth's interior and surface.
A square kilometer measures area. A liter measures volume. A kilogram measures mass. A second measures time.
Beavers build dams using branches, mud, and stones. Otters have dense fur that traps insulating air. Badgers dig extensive underground burrows. Squirrels use sharp claws for climbing.
A fountain pen delivers liquid ink through a nib. A pencil leaves graphite on paper. A marker uses a porous tip to apply ink. Chalk leaves fine mineral particles on rough surfaces.
Apples develop from flowers on deciduous trees. Pineapples consist of many fused individual fruits. Raspberries are clusters of small drupelets. Watermelons grow on trailing vines.
A calendar organizes days, weeks, and months. A clock measures and displays time. A stopwatch measures elapsed time intervals. An hourglass tracks time using flowing granular material.

Polar bears have black skin beneath translucent fur. Camels conserve water through several physiological adaptations. Arctic foxes develop dense seasonal coats. Fennec foxes have large ears that release heat.
A bridge spans an obstacle such as water or a valley. A tunnel provides passage through earth or rock. A dam controls water flow across a river. A lighthouse displays navigational light near a coast.

An orchestra includes musicians playing several instrument families. A choir performs music using coordinated voices. A quartet contains four performers or musical parts. A solo features one principal performer.

Rainbows arise from refraction, reflection, and dispersion in water droplets. Mirages result from light bending through air layers. Shadows form where an object blocks light. Glare comes from intense reflected or direct light.
A bee has three main body sections. A spider has eight legs and two main body regions. A centipede has one pair of legs per body segment. A crab has a hard external skeleton and jointed limbs.

Brick walls use repeated masonry units joined with mortar. Steel frames support many large modern buildings. Timber beams provide structural support in wooden construction. Reinforced concrete contains embedded steel bars.

A keyboard instrument produces notes through keys. A string instrument uses vibrating strings as sound sources. A wind instrument uses moving air to generate sound. A percussion instrument sounds through striking, shaking, or scraping.

Satellites orbit planets or other larger bodies. Meteors are luminous streaks caused by atmospheric entry. Meteorites are fragments that reach a planetary surface. Nebulae are clouds of gas and dust in space.

A robin has an orange-red breast in many populations. A peacock displays elongated colorful upper tail coverts. A flamingo obtains pink pigments from its diet. A magpie often has contrasting black and white plumage.

An archive preserves records with long-term value. A database stores structured information for retrieval. A notebook holds handwritten or printed notes. A catalog organizes descriptions of available items.

Hydrogen is the most abundant element in the universe. Oxygen supports ordinary combustion reactions. Nitrogen makes up most of Earth's atmosphere. Helium is chemically inert under ordinary conditions.

A violin bridge transfers string vibrations to the body. A guitar soundboard amplifies vibrations from the strings. A trumpet bell radiates sound into the surrounding air. A clarinet reed initiates vibration at the mouthpiece.
Peaches have fuzzy skin and a central stone. Oranges contain juice-filled segments inside a rind. Cherries grow as small stone fruits on trees. Kiwifruit has green flesh with many tiny seeds.
A raven belongs to the crow family. A swan belongs to the waterfowl family. A penguin belongs to a group of flightless seabirds. A hummingbird belongs to a family specialized for hovering flight.
A capacitor stores electrical charge in an electric field. An inductor stores energy in a magnetic field. A resistor limits electric current. A diode favors current flow in one direction.
Clay becomes moldable when mixed with water. Porcelain is fired at high temperatures. Terracotta usually retains a reddish natural color. Stoneware is dense and durable after firing.

A meadow is dominated by grasses and flowering plants. A forest contains a high density of trees. A tundra supports low vegetation in cold climates. A marsh is a wetland dominated by herbaceous plants.
A bee orchid flower resembles certain insects. A sunflower head contains many small individual flowers. A tulip grows from an underground bulb. A lotus roots in mud while leaves float above water.
A violin case protects the instrument during storage. A music stand supports printed scores. A metronome provides a regular pulse. A tuning fork produces a stable reference pitch.

Saturn's rings contain countless particles of ice and rock. Jupiter's Great Red Spot is a giant atmospheric storm. Mars has polar caps containing water ice and carbon dioxide ice. Venus is covered by thick clouds of sulfuric acid droplets.
A red traffic light signals vehicles to stop. A pedestrian crossing marks a route across a roadway. A speed limit sign states a legal maximum speed. A stop sign requires drivers to halt.
Paper clips hold sheets together using spring tension. Staples fasten paper with bent metal legs. Glue bonds surfaces through adhesion. Tape joins surfaces with a pressure-sensitive coating.
A salmon has fins supported by bony rays. A squid moves partly by jet propulsion. A lobster has large claws and a segmented body. A sea star uses tube feet for movement.

A compass needle aligns approximately with Earth's magnetic field. A gyroscope resists changes to its rotation axis. An accelerometer measures changes in velocity. A level indicates whether a surface is horizontal or vertical.
Rye is a cereal grain tolerant of cool climates. Chickpeas are protein-rich legumes. Barley is used in foods and fermented drinks. Millet includes several small-seeded cereal crops.

A violin can produce sustained tones with continuous bowing. A piano note begins with a percussive hammer strike. A flute tone depends on an air jet at an edge. A drum tone decays after the membrane is struck.
An octopus has eight flexible arms. A jellyfish lacks a backbone and true brain. A sea urchin has movable spines on a hard shell. A clam protects its soft body with two hinged shells.

Red paint reflects more red light than other visible wavelengths. Black surfaces absorb much visible light. White surfaces reflect many visible wavelengths. Transparent glass transmits much visible light.
